description_long: This object is a medieval-style sword featuring a long, straight, double-edged steel blade with a rounded tip. The blade has a slightly weathered, matte metallic finish, suggesting frequent use or age. The crossguard is simple and straight, made of the same metal as the blade, providing basic hand protection. The handle is cylindrical and wrapped in a brown material, likely leather, for improved grip and comfort. The pommel at the end of the handle is faceted and metallic, serving as a counterweight for balance. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements, making it suitable for practical use rather than ceremonial display.
description: A medieval-style sword with a long, straight, double-edged steel blade, a simple crossguard, and a brown leather-wrapped handle with a faceted metal pommel.
description_view_0: The sword is seen edge-on, showing the thin profile of the blade and the cylindrical handle.
description_view_1: The full flat of the blade is visible, highlighting the double-edged design, straight crossguard, and brown handle wrap.
description_view_2: Another edge-on view, similar to view 0, showing the opposite side of the handle and blade.
description_view_3: A mirrored flat view of the blade, crossguard, and handle, similar to view 1 but from the reverse side.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, brown leather-wrapped, faceted metal pommel
